verses.text: 'Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.' By it 'we understand that the worlds were framed.' What faith is. Faith gives present grip on unseen realities.
'Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.' By it 'we understand that the worlds were framed.' What faith is. Faith gives present grip on unseen realities.
'Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.' By it 'we understand that the worlds were framed.' What faith is. Faith gives present grip on unseen realities.
By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice; by faith Enoch 'was translated that he should not see death,' pleasing God. Walking with God. Faith both worships rightly and walks closely.
By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice; by faith Enoch 'was translated that he should not see death,' pleasing God. Walking with God. Faith both worships rightly and walks closely.
'Without faith it is impossible to please him'; by faith Noah, warned of God, prepared an ark. Faith obeys. The believer trusts and acts on God's word unseen.
'Without faith it is impossible to please him'; by faith Noah, warned of God, prepared an ark. Faith obeys. The believer trusts and acts on God's word unseen.
By faith Abraham obeyed, going out 'not knowing whither,' looking for 'a city... whose builder and maker is God.' Pilgrim faith. He left the seen for the promised.
By faith Abraham obeyed, going out 'not knowing whither,' looking for 'a city... whose builder and maker is God.' Pilgrim faith. He left the seen for the promised.
By faith Abraham obeyed, going out 'not knowing whither,' looking for 'a city... whose builder and maker is God.' Pilgrim faith. He left the seen for the promised.
Through faith Sara received strength to conceive, judging Him faithful who promised; of one came a countless seed. God keeps promise. Faith counts on God's faithfulness.
Through faith Sara received strength to conceive, judging Him faithful who promised; of one came a countless seed. God keeps promise. Faith counts on God's faithfulness.
These died in faith, 'strangers and pilgrims on the earth,' seeking 'a better country, that is, an heavenly.' Heaven-bound. They held the world loosely, longing for home.
These died in faith, 'strangers and pilgrims on the earth,' seeking 'a better country, that is, an heavenly.' Heaven-bound. They held the world loosely, longing for home.
These died in faith, 'strangers and pilgrims on the earth,' seeking 'a better country, that is, an heavenly.' Heaven-bound. They held the world loosely, longing for home.
These died in faith, 'strangers and pilgrims on the earth,' seeking 'a better country, that is, an heavenly.' Heaven-bound. They held the world loosely, longing for home.
By faith Abraham offered Isaac, 'accounting that God was able to raise him up.' Tested trust. Faith obeys even the hardest command, sure of God's power.
By faith Abraham offered Isaac, 'accounting that God was able to raise him up.' Tested trust. Faith obeys even the hardest command, sure of God's power.
By faith Abraham offered Isaac, 'accounting that God was able to raise him up.' Tested trust. Faith obeys even the hardest command, sure of God's power.
By faith Isaac, Jacob, and Joseph blessed and spoke of things to come, even of the carrying up of their bones. Dying faith. They believed God's promise beyond their own lives.
By faith Isaac, Jacob, and Joseph blessed and spoke of things to come, even of the carrying up of their bones. Dying faith. They believed God's promise beyond their own lives.
By faith Isaac, Jacob, and Joseph blessed and spoke of things to come, even of the carrying up of their bones. Dying faith. They believed God's promise beyond their own lives.
By faith Moses' parents hid him; he refused Pharaoh's court, 'esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches.' Costly choice. Faith prizes Christ above the world's treasures.
By faith Moses' parents hid him; he refused Pharaoh's court, 'esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches.' Costly choice. Faith prizes Christ above the world's treasures.
By faith Moses' parents hid him; he refused Pharaoh's court, 'esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches.' Costly choice. Faith prizes Christ above the world's treasures.
By faith Moses' parents hid him; he refused Pharaoh's court, 'esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches.' Costly choice. Faith prizes Christ above the world's treasures.
By faith he forsook Egypt, kept the passover, and passed through the Red sea as on dry land, 'seeing him who is invisible.' Bold faith. Faith endures by sight of the unseen God.
By faith he forsook Egypt, kept the passover, and passed through the Red sea as on dry land, 'seeing him who is invisible.' Bold faith. Faith endures by sight of the unseen God.
By faith he forsook Egypt, kept the passover, and passed through the Red sea as on dry land, 'seeing him who is invisible.' Bold faith. Faith endures by sight of the unseen God.
By faith the walls of Jericho fell; by faith Rahab the harlot was spared, having received the spies. Saving faith. Even an outsider's faith brings deliverance.
By faith the walls of Jericho fell; by faith Rahab the harlot was spared, having received the spies. Saving faith. Even an outsider's faith brings deliverance.
Time fails to tell of Gideon, David, and the prophets, who 'through faith subdued kingdoms,' and women 'received their dead raised.' Faith's triumphs. By faith the weak were made strong.
Time fails to tell of Gideon, David, and the prophets, who 'through faith subdued kingdoms,' and women 'received their dead raised.' Faith's triumphs. By faith the weak were made strong.
Time fails to tell of Gideon, David, and the prophets, who 'through faith subdued kingdoms,' and women 'received their dead raised.' Faith's triumphs. By faith the weak were made strong.
Time fails to tell of Gideon, David, and the prophets, who 'through faith subdued kingdoms,' and women 'received their dead raised.' Faith's triumphs. By faith the weak were made strong.
Others were tortured, scourged, stoned, sawn asunder, wandering in deserts, 'of whom the world was not worthy.' Faith's sufferings. Faith may lead to loss as well as victory.
Others were tortured, scourged, stoned, sawn asunder, wandering in deserts, 'of whom the world was not worthy.' Faith's sufferings. Faith may lead to loss as well as victory.
Others were tortured, scourged, stoned, sawn asunder, wandering in deserts, 'of whom the world was not worthy.' Faith's sufferings. Faith may lead to loss as well as victory.
All these obtained a good report, yet received not the promise, 'God having provided some better thing for us.' Better together. The faithful of all ages are perfected together in Christ.
All these obtained a good report, yet received not the promise, 'God having provided some better thing for us.' Better together. The faithful of all ages are perfected together in Christ.
